title: The Behavior of Uncertainty and Disagreement and Their Roles in Economic Prediction: A Panel Analysis

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: This staff report examines point and density forecasts from the European Central Bank's Survey of Professional Forecasters, deriving individual uncertainty measures and disagreement measures. The authors find substantial heterogeneity in respondents' uncertainty and disagreement, with little co-movement between the two. Forecast performance shows a more robust inverse relationship with disagreement than with uncertainty, and forecast revisions display a stronger association with disagreement.
key_points: Source is a Federal Reserve Bank of New York Staff Report (Number 808), published February 2017
Uses European Central Bank's Survey of Professional Forecasters (SPF) data as the primary dataset
Authors derive individual uncertainty measures and point/density-based measures of disagreement
Key finding: substantial heterogeneity exists in respondents' uncertainty and disagreement
Little co-movement observed between uncertainty and disagreement measures
Forecast performance shows stronger inverse relationship with disagreement than with uncertainty
Respondents with higher disagreement levels make larger forecast revisions
why_it_matters: This research is relevant for understanding how professional forecasters form expectations, particularly the distinction between uncertainty (individual forecaster's doubt) and disagreement (inter-forecasters divergence). The findings suggest disagreement may be a more reliable signal for forecast quality and revision behavior than uncertainty alone.
